title: Secret Bytes, Constant-Time Decisions, and Memory Lifecycle · Runtime Contract: Crypto Go Side Channel Scope
summary: A Go service converts secrets to immutable strings, logs parse failures with raw material, compares tags with ordinary equality, and promises memory erasure the runtime cannot prove. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the side channel scope increment in secret custody and comparison protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, c…
objectives: Identify timing, cache, branch, allocation, power, fault, error, and length channels and state which are or are not tested.
Use constant-time comparison APIs for fixed-length secret-dependent values and reject length or parse errors without secret-dependent detail.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Predict Crypto Go Side Channel Scope · Theory Crypto Go Side Channel Scope
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory cryptographic engineering team handles case 7866 during a replicated device restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound secret copies, constant-time comparison scope, redaction, and honest erasure claims; keep browser execution deterministic and move production entropy, native timing, non-allowlisted crypto, network, HSM, and compliance claims to authorized transfer gates; then change one authenticated byte and prove clean rejection without partial effect. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Crypto Go Side Channel Scope contract for the supplied data.
